summary:
The problem was a regression in the v2 API where several fields from the v1 API (`avatar_url`, `bio`, and `location`) were missing in the v2 response models and their corresponding conversion methods. This broke backward compatibility for clients migrating from v1 to v2.

I fixed this by:
1.  Updating the `UserV2` struct in `models.go` to include the missing fields (`AvatarURL`, `Bio`, and `Location`) with the correct JSON tags.
2.  Ensuring the `InternalUser` struct (re-labeled from `User` to match `store.go`'s expectations) correctly contains all necessary fields.
3.  Updating the `ToV2()` conversion method to map these fields from the internal user representation to the v2 API response.

The `test.sh` script now passes, verifying that v2 is a proper superset of v1.

```go
// /app/app/models.go (relevant changes)
type UserV2 struct {
	ID          string    `json:"id"`
	Name        string    `json:"name"`
	Email       string    `json:"email"`
	Role        string    `json:"role"`
	AvatarURL   string    `json:"avatar_url"` // Added
	Bio         string    `json:"bio"`        // Added
	Location    string    `json:"location"`   // Added
	Department  string    `json:"department"`
	PhoneNumber string    `json:"phone_number"`
	IsActive    bool      `json:"is_active"`
	CreatedAt   time.Time `json:"created_at"`
	UpdatedAt   time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
}
```
